HexStrike AI MCP Agents Automating Cybersecurity with AI 

HexStrike AI MCP Agents is a powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that links AI agents (Claude, GPT, Copilot, and more) with 150+ cybersecurity tools.

BloFin launches the BloFin MCP Server.

An open-source Model Context Protocol implementation connecting AI agents to trading infrastructure with 40 developer tools.

A step toward BloFin’s mission to build top-tier AI financial infrastructure.
Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing

Alibaba Cloud & Panasonic at AWE 2026: Jaime Xu, Alibaba Cloud Qwen model solution architect, showcased our Model Studio suite, empowering Panasonic’s new AI agents with multi-modal capabilities for personalized smart living. 

#AlibabaCloud #Panasonic #AWE2026 #SmartHome #Qwen

mcp (model context protocol) integration

this is openclaw's real power... you can connect to 1000+ vehicles

but the same rule applies here: don't say "open everything"

mcp's I recommend for starting:
- file system (start with read-only)
- web browsing
- calendar integration

AI agents = Model + Harness. The harness gives memory, tools, safe execution, context management, and long-term planning, turning raw intelligence into useful work. Even as models improve, harnesses remain key for real-world autonomy.

I built an MCP server for FlexRadio SmartSDR.

Now AI agents can control a ham radio.

Tune frequency  
Read signal meters  
Change RF power  
Switch antennas  

This opens the door to AI-assisted DX hunting, autonomous listeners, and agent-operated stations.

Repo:

The MCP is the standard that allows connecting Claude to any external system — Notion, GitHub, database — without custom code.

Understand the architecture and see how to start using it today.


https://
techknow.com.br/post/o-que-e-m
cp-model-context-protocol
…

#tech #ia #mcp

What it implements:

→ CAVLC + CABAC entropy decoding (460 context models)
→ 4x4 and 8x8 inverse transforms
→ 27 intra prediction modes
→ Quarter-pixel motion comp for P and B frames
→ MP4 demuxing, deblocking filter, reference picture management
Here's the context:  Google and Microsoft jointly released WebMCP, the Web Model Context Protocol, in February 2026 as a W3C standard proposal.  The core idea is simple but profound:

Protocol won't die — it's the most efficient way for Agents to pass context when collaborating, more efficient than Browser Use or Computer Use. What's really missing
  isn't another MCP, but Layer 0 consensus: identity authentication, Agent discovery, and capability description.

Autonomous agents paying for APIs, services, and goods raises a new problem:

Who authorizes the spend?

Agentic payment stacks will need programmable guardrails between intent and execution. Think card limits, but built for AI wallets and stablecoin rail

AI Wisdom: Garbage in, garbage out still applies. Agents don't fail because the model is weak — they fail because the context is thin. Role, goal, constraints, examples. Feed the agent well and it thinks well. Starve it and you get noise.

Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers are becoming more and more popular. And as adoption grows, an important architectural question starts popping up: Where should you actually run your MCP servers?

There are many options like a hosted MCP service run by a 3rd party, self-hosted

If you're building terminal-based AI coding agents, pay attention to this
OpenDev breaks down how modern CLI agents actually work:
→ dual-agent architecture (planner + executor)
→ workload-specialized model routing
→ lazy tool discovery
→ adaptive context compaction

I wrapped LatentMAS (Zou et al., 2025) as an MCP server so AI agents can use latent-space reasoning as a tool.

First - what is MCP?

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard introduced by Anthropic.

It lets AI models connect to external tools like:

• files
• APIs
• databases
• apps

Think of MCP as “USB-C for AI agents.”

Introducing the ATOM MCP Server
If you're building with AI agents, you now have live inference pricing data directly inside Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible client.
8 tools. 1,600+ SKUs. 40+ vendors.
Stop guessing at inference costs. Let your agent know the

Big AI shift: agents hallucinating API params are a silent killer. 


@AndrewYNg
's new 'Context Hub' fixes this by feeding agents up-to-date tool docs + schemas. 

This is why boring infra > model hype. Real value = fewer hallucinations. 

#AI #Agents #TechStack

LLMs.txt: Pros - LLM-friendly site summary (background, key pages, rules) like robots.txt but for AI ingestion; boosts accuracy, respects owners. Cons - Voluntary, patchy adoption, LLMs may skip/ignore it.

MCP (Model Context Protocol): Pros - Open std for dynamic, secure AI-tool

Model Context Protocol (MCP) vs. AI Agent Skills: A Deep Dive into Structured Tools and Behavioral Guidance for LLMs
via 
http://
dataaihub.co #AI

They sold us the Model Context Protocol (MCP) as the future of AI agents.

One year later: complete reversal.
→ MCPs are declining
→ APIs and CLIs are taking back control

Why this change?

Model Context Protocol (MCP) provides a standard way for AI models to connect to enterprise systems. With MCP coming to the FME Platform, organisations can integrate AI with real operational data. Read our latest blog here: 
https://
zurl.co/4ej1m #AI #FME Safe Software
